lines.en_lit: because it resembles the luster of pearls [paz] Shaḥut gold because is spun like thread [shenitve keḥut]. Closed gold because when opens all the shops close, Parvayim gold because resembles the blood of bulls [parim].

Rav Ashi said: There are five each and every one gold and good gold. That was also taught On every day of greenish gold, but this day a red and this is the parvayim gold which resembles the blood of bulls.

On every day, sacrificed a peras, in the morning, On every day, was fine but this day it was superfine. The Sages taught “finely ground What verse teach Has it not already been stated: “And you shall grind some of it finely” Rather, it teaches superfine.
On every day, priests ascend on the eastern side of the ramp. As the Master said: All the turns that you turn should be only to the right, to the east
But this day ascend in the middle and descend in the middle. What is the reason? Due to the eminence High Priest
every day the High Priest sanctifies his hands and his feet from the laver What is the reason? Due to the eminence High Priest.

On every day, there were four arrangements there, The Sages taught On every day there were two arrangements but this day three: One, large arrangement; and one, second arrangement for the incense; and one, which they add on that day the statement of Rabbi Yehuda.

Rabbi Yosei says: On every day three but this day four: One, the large arrangement; and one, second arrangement for the incense; and one, for the upkeep of the fire, and one, that they add on that day

Rabbi Meir says: On every day there were four but this day five: One, large arrangement; and one, second arrangement for the incense; and one, for the upkeep of the fire; one, for the limbs and fats that were not consumed the evening; and one, that they add on that day

At any rate, everyone has two From where do we The verse states: “It is the burnt-offering on the flame on the altar all night” this the large arrangement. “And the fire of the altar shall be kept burning thereby” this the second arrangement, for the incense. And from where does Rabbi Yosei for the upkeep of the fire? He derives it “And the fire upon the altar shall be kept burning thereby”

And Rabbi Yehuda, That comes to the kindling thin wood chips, as it was taught Rabbi Yehuda would say: From where that the kindling of the wood chips should only at the top of the altar, The verse states: “And the fire upon the altar shall be kept burning thereby” Rabbi Yosei said: From where that an arrangement for the upkeep of the fire is made? The verse states: “And the fire upon the altar shall be kept burning thereby”

And from where Rabbi Yosei kindling of the wood chips He derives it from the place Rabbi Shimon derives it. As it was taught “And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar” teaches about the kindling of wood chips that it may be only by a fit priest and in vestments of service; the statement of Rabbi Yehuda. Rabbi Shimon said to him: for could it enter your mind that a non-priest could approach the altar? Rather, teaches kindling of the wood chips, that they may be lit only at the top of the altar.
And Rabbi Yehuda If from there, I would have said standing on the ground and using a bellows teaches us
And from where does Rabbi Meir limbs and the fats that were not consumed the evening? He derives it “and the fire.” And the Rabbis, do not expound “and.”

And the Rabbis, what do they do with the limbs and the fats that were not consumed the evening? returns them to the large arrangement, As it was taught From where for limbs and fats that were not consumed the evening,
